title: The Emerald Tablet
author: Hermes Trismegistus
description: The first ink was drawn from the depths of the obsidian springs, marking the birth of a collection that would outlast empires and stars alike...
longDescription: This manuscript, recovered from the brine-chilled depths of the Vesperal Library, contains the only known maps of the stars as they appeared before the Great Shifting. Elara's prose is as dense as the dark matter she describes, weaving together mathematical certainties with theological warnings that still resonate in the modern silence.

title: De Revolutionibus Orbium Coelestium
author: Nicolaus Copernicus
description: A revolutionary astronomical work that placed the Sun at the center of the universe.

title: Chronicon Pontificum et Imperatorum
author: Martin of Opava
description: A comprehensive chronicle of popes and emperors, serving as a vital historical record.

title: The Lesser Key of Solomon
author: Anonymous (Solomonic Tradition)
description: An anonymous grimoire on demonology, compiled from earlier materials.

title: Philosophiæ Naturalis Principia Mathematica
author: Isaac Newton
description: The foundation of classical mechanics and the law of universal gravitation.

title: The Histories of Herodotus
author: Herodotus
description: The founding work of history in Western literature, investigating the origins of the Greco-Persian Wars.

title: The Voynich Manuscript
author: Unknown Author
description: An illustrated codex hand-written in an unknown writing system.

title: Ars Combinatoria
author: Gottfried Wilhelm Leibniz
description: An early work on computing and the alphabet of human thought.
